bead.title: frontend: verify Story 16 run-detail p95 tab interactivity
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Story 16.3 part 3 needs the run-detail page to stay responsive with a p95-shaped fixture: 200 tool calls and 30 evidence files. The prior bead text accidentally included copied parent scope for FEAT-008/FEAT-010/FEAT-019 spec updates, server audit events, and broader cross-cutting e2e work, but this child owns only the p95 frontend/e2e verification slice.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/runs.spec.ts:1164 defines the intended `Story16RunDetailFullStackP95` Playwright scenario.
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/runs.spec.ts:1367 defines the one-render-frame interaction guard via `requestAnimationFrame` and a `<100ms` assertion.
- The previous decomposition copied parent context into this child, leaving the title/description broader than the acceptance criteria.

PROPOSED FIX
- Keep this bead scoped to the `Story16RunDetailFullStackP95` Playwright coverage.
- Verify the prompt, response, tools, and evidence tabs render expected content for a fixture with 200 tool calls and 30 evidence files.
- Verify those tabs remain interactive within one `requestAnimationFrame` after mocked GraphQL payload resolution.
- Preserve the standard e2e, Go, and lefthook gates.

NON-SCOPE
- FEAT-008, FEAT-010, or FEAT-019 spec prose updates.
- Server audit-event implementation.
- New auth or RBAC behavior.
- Parent or sibling acceptance criteria outside this p95 frontend/e2e slice.
bead.acceptance:
1. `test('Story16RunDetailFullStackP95', ...)` in `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/runs.spec.ts` uses a fixture with 200 tool calls and 30 evidence files.
2. The test verifies prompt, response, tools, and evidence tabs render expected content and remain interactive within one `requestAnimationFrame` after mocked GraphQL payload resolution.
3. `bun run test:e2e` passes.
4. `cd cli && go test ./...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

{"rationale":"Bead has structural problems: title says 'part 3' covering 'Specs + cross-cutting e2e + raw-transcript-viewed audit event' but ACs only cover p95 perf rendering, which doesn't match the title scope. Description has duplicated PROBLEM/ROOT CAUSE/PROPOSED FIX/NON-SCOPE sections (parent context bleed). AC #1 references 'all tabs' and 'render frame' without naming a specific test function or perf harness. No AC verifies the spec updates (FEAT-008/010/019), audit event, or cross-cutting e2e named in title/description. Root cause cites spec files but not file:line. Has parent + deps. Labels include phase/story but missing area:* and kind:*.","score":4,"suggested_fixes":["Reconcile title with ACs — either narrow title to 'p95 perf budget for run-detail tabs' or expand ACs to cover spec updates, audit event, and cross-cutting e2e","Remove duplicated PROBLEM/ROOT CAUSE/PROPOSED FIX/NON-SCOPE blocks; keep one coherent set scoped to this child","Name specific Test*/Playwright spec function in AC #1 (e.g., 'tests/e2e/run-detail-perf.spec.ts: renders p95 dataset within frame budget')","Add ACs that verify each title element: spec doc updates land, 'raw transcript viewed' audit event emitted (assert via server test), cross-cutting e2e exists","Add area:* and kind:* labels","Cite spec files with line ranges or section anchors rather than glob paths"],"waivers_applied":[]}

The acceptance criteria only cover the performance slice, leaving it ambiguous whether the spec updates, audit event, and cross-cutting e2e are in scope for this child or assigned to siblings. {"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"executable scope is internally consistent: The bead is not safely executable as written because the body contains two conflicting slices. The first PROPOSED FIX assigns specs, resolver-contract docs, a raw-transcript-viewed audit event, and cross-cutting e2e coverage; the later 'Implement only this child acceptance slice' and acceptance criteria assign only a p95 render/interactivity performance target plus test gates. That is not a narrow prose issue: an implementer would have to choose between documentation/audit/e2e scope and performance scope. {"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"scope coherence between description and ac: Description and acceptance criteria are misaligned. The description claims this child owns FEAT-008/FEAT-010/FEAT-019 spec updates, a new 'raw transcript viewed' audit event in server+schema, and a cross-cutting Playwright e2e — but the acceptance criteria only require a p95 performance check (200 tool calls, 30 evidence files render within one render frame) plus standard test gates. An agent has no way to know whether to implement the full prose scope or only the AC slice. Additionally, the 'one render frame' performance target has no defined measurement harness (what counts as 'interactive', how it is measured, where the dataset is fixtured), making AC #1 not verifiable as written. The description also contains duplicated 'PROBLEM' / 'PROPOSED FIX' / 'NON-SCOPE' headers from a botched decomposition, which compounds the ambiguity about which scope is authoritative.","fingerprint":"4a4c61c4c495026c402b5fc3d74cf2535cc4223730d9e55d287891592f499c0c","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment"}

{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"description scope is unambiguous: Description contains two stacked PROBLEM/ROOT CAUSE/PROPOSED FIX/NON-SCOPE blocks: an outer block describing parent scope (FEAT-008/010/019 spec updates, 'raw transcript viewed' audit event, cross-cutting Playwright e2e) and an inner block narrowing this child to only p95 perf + e2e green + lefthook. An executor reading cold cannot tell which scope is theirs — the description claims spec edits and an audit event are in scope, but AC only lists perf + green test suites. AC item 1 is unmeasurable as written: 'within one render frame' has no numeric budget (16ms? 33ms? p95 over how many samples?), no named perf test, no file:line for where to add it, and no instrumentation strategy for 'become interactive'. No Test* function or Playwright spec name is wired to AC item 2. Dependencies on siblings ddx-868ac26c/ddx-f129bfaf are listed but their scope split is not stated, so the executor cannot tell whether the cross-cutting e2e in the outer PROPOSED FIX is owned here or by a sibling.","fingerprint":"4a4c61c4c495026c402b5fc3d74cf2535cc4223730d9e55d287891592f499c0c","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment"}

{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"title_matches_acceptance: Title advertises three deliverables (spec updates to FEAT-008/010/019, cross-cutting e2e, raw-transcript-viewed audit event) but the child acceptance slice only contains a p95 performance check (200 tool calls, 30 evidence files render within 'one render frame'). The description carries the parent's full PROBLEM/ROOT CAUSE/PROPOSED FIX block verbatim, then redefines the child scope to a single perf criterion, leaving it ambiguous whether this part-3 child owns the specs+audit+e2e work or only the perf budget. 'One render frame' is not defined (16ms? 33ms?) and no file:line names where to instrument or measure. No Test* function or playwright spec path is named. Sibling split (parts 1/2/3) is not described, so an executor cannot tell which AC items are owned here vs. by ddx-868ac26c / ddx-f129bfaf.","fingerprint":"4a4c61c4c495026c402b5fc3d74cf2535cc4223730d9e55d287891592f499c0c","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment"}

{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"scope vs ac mismatch: Title says 'part 3' but description mixes two distinct scopes: (a) spec updates to FEAT-008/010/019 plus a new 'raw transcript viewed' audit event and cross-cutting e2e, and (b) a p95 performance slice (200 tool calls, 30 evidence files, one render frame). Acceptance criteria only cover the performance slice, leaving the spec edits and audit event unverified and orphaned. Agent cannot tell whether to edit specs, add the audit event, write the e2e, or just hit the perf budget — and whether sibling children already own the spec work.
